nodes.label: Direction 2 — SCFF/GD interleave
nodes.rationale: Phase 2 already half-answered this and the answer is mostly 'don't' — GD layers interleaved mid-stream fail; the one live seam is a non-collapsing intermediate objective, lowest priority of the three directions.

nodes.label: P2.5 read vs write result
nodes.rationale: 'read' (GD layer as readout only, raw SCFF features still flow forward) works (+0.010, ~85% of GD accuracy at ~1/6 backward); 'write' (GD's class-aligned rep fed forward into next SCFF block) failed decisively (-0.04 to -0.09, worse with more checkpoints).

nodes.label: Class-collapse mechanism (why write fails)
nodes.rationale: A GD layer trained to predict logits produces a class-collapsed representation that throws away everything except the class direction; SCFF on top of a collapsed rep must re-cluster an information-starved input — the stream must carry rich SCFF features, never a class-collapsed GD rep.

nodes.label: Non-collapsing intermediate objective (predsim-style)
nodes.rationale: predsim's sim (similarity-matching) loss keeps a high-dimensional structure-preserving target instead of collapsing to logits — the untested variant that might interleave where CE-write can't; folded later into Direction 1/3 rather than its own track.
